title: aira tenorei subsp. mixta
intro: Aira tenorei subsp. mixta, also known as mixed hair grass or sea couch, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Aira tenorei subsp. mixta include its ability to ease congestion, soothe sore throats, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable herb for respiratory health. Aira tenorei subsp. mixta can be prepared as an infusion by steeping the dried grass in hot water or as a decoction by boiling the roots and leaves to create a tea for medicinal purposes.
